diagrams.description: This timeline traces key milestones of the History of the Americas from the first human migrations to recent times. Major dates include: c. 20,000 BCE first peopling via Beringia; c. 1500 BCE‑300 CE Olmec civilisation; 1492 Columbus’s arrival; 1519‑1521 conquest of Mexico; 1532 conquest of Peru; 1607 Jamestown founded; 1776 United States Declaration of Independence; 1804 Haitian independence; 1810‑1825 Spanish American independence; 1861‑1865 US Civil War and abolition of slavery; 1888 abolition of slavery in Brazil; 1910‑1920 Mexican Revolution; 1959 Cuban Revolution; 1960s‑70s civil rights movements; 1970s‑80s military dictatorships in South America; 2006 election of Evo Morales in Bolivia; and 2020 pandemic‑era migration and equity debates.
diagrams.mermaid_code:
timeline title Key Milestones in the History of the Americas section Early Migrations & Pre‑Columbian c. 20,000 BCE : First human migrations
via Beringia c. 1500‑300 BCE : Olmec civilisation 250‑900 CE : Classic Maya period 1325 : Founding of Tenochtitlan (Aztec) 1438‑1533 : Inca Empire expansion section Conquest & Colonisation 1492 : Columbus reaches Caribbean 1519‑1521 : Cortés conquers Aztec Empire 1532 : Pizarro conquers Inca Empire 1607 : Jamestown (first permanent English settlement) section Independence & Nation‑Building 1776 : US Declaration of Independence 1804 : Haitian independence 1810‑1825 : Spanish American independence wars 1823 : Monroe Doctrine 1861‑1865 : US Civil War; abolition of slavery in US 1888 : Abolition of slavery in Brazil section Revolutions & Social Change 1910‑1920 : Mexican Revolution 1917 : Mexican Constitution 1959 : Cuban Revolution 1960s‑70s : Civil rights movements; indigenous activism 1970s‑80s : Military dictatorships in South America section Contemporary Era 1994 : NAFTA; Zapatista uprising (Mexico) 2006 : Evo Morales elected in Bolivia 2020 : Pandemic; renewed racial equity protests
across the hemisphere
